patches.find: The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path.
patches.replace: The 2009 July 22 eclipse's path of totality crossed central China through the Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai corridor with full obscuration along a ~258 km-wide band; coverage at Chinese sites further north or west (e.g., Beijing, Changchun, Lhasa, Urumqi) was substantial partial rather than totality. The cited Chinese SG campaign's eight gravimeters were not necessarily uniformly on that path — the cited 2013 CJG record does not publish a per-site coverage table we have located in English — and a defender pivot would argue that off-totality stations should not have shown a κ-coupled Δg because their local ΔB was scaled down by partial coverage. Two structural rebuttals foreclose this pivot. First, the dome publishes no per-site coverage threshold for κ: κ = 1.677 nT/µGal is presented as a constant, not a coverage-gated function — so a coverage-scaling carve-out is a post-hoc scope condition the dome would have to publish in advance to claim. Second, the arithmetic survives the scaling: even at 70% partial coverage, a proportionally-scaled ΔB of 3.5–10.5 nT through κ still predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal at the off-totality SG sites, 2× to 60× above the 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction floor; the network-wide null thus refutes κ-coupling at any plausible coverage scaling, not only at full totality. Any coverage threshold consistent with exempting 2009 also exempts much of the 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble (Kim & Chang 2018) the dome itself invokes for WIN-068.
patches.rationale: EXP-349 closes ISS-1959: tightens loose China coverage claim with precise totality-corridor geography and forecloses off-path partial-coverage defender pivot via two structural rebuttals.

patches.find: fundamental cavity resonance at ~0.018 Hz, not 7.83 Hz
patches.replace: fundamental cavity resonance at ~8.78 Hz (quarter-wave at H_max = 8,537 km), not 7.83 Hz
patches.rationale: WIN-034.c3 critical: 0.018 Hz is wrong by ~500x. Correct quarter-wave resonance at H_max=8,537 km is c/(4H)=299,792/(4*8537)=8.78 Hz, consistent with WIN-038.

patches.find: fundamental cavity resonance ~0.018 Hz, not the observed 7.83 Hz
patches.replace: fundamental cavity resonance ~8.78 Hz (quarter-wave at H_max = 8,537 km), not the observed 7.83 Hz
patches.rationale: WIN-034.c3 critical: same 0.018 Hz error in finding field.

patches.find: derives ~0.018 Hz from the dome's own H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619), not the observed 7.83 Hz
patches.replace: derives ~8.78 Hz (quarter-wave at H_max = 8,537 km) from the dome's own H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619), not the observed 7.83 Hz
patches.rationale: WIN-034.c3 critical: same 0.018 Hz error in tldr_verdict field.

patches.find: Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test — though for a different reason than the dome’s numerical WINs.
patches.replace: Note on verdict framing: the Self-Contradicted verdict is primary, derived from the dome’s load-bearing use of this physical specification in WIN-029 and WIN-048, where its own H(r) at H_max produces ~8.78 Hz — not 7.83 Hz — under structural-coherence testing. Separately and independently, this claim also meets our post-hoc unfalsifiability flag — though for a different reason than the dome’s numerical WINs.
patches.rationale: WIN-034.c3 major: detail_verdict_text led with Unfalsifiable framing contradicting SC verdict. Added clarifying note that SC is primary, unfalsifiable is secondary.

SEC-part2-sign-only-EXP337.c1 critical: 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 resolves to a CRISPR paper (Kim et al. 2020), not Livermore et al. 2020. Correct DOI is 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 (Nature Geoscience, already used in WIN-059 prose).
